entry.title: Re: See a complete end to end process diagram in signavio
entry.content:
Hi @jigar_patel42 

In SAP Signavio Process Manager, there is no automatic “full end‑to‑end” flattened view that expands all linked subprocess diagrams into one combined diagram. Linked subprocesses can only be opened individually via the “+” icon in the model or Collaboration Hub, rather than displayed as a single integrated diagram. 

If you want everything in one visual, the only option is to model a high‑level diagram using expanded subprocesses, but this must be built manually. Here's the documentation that can help you with creating sub-processes - https://help.sap.com/docs/signavio-process-manager/user-guide/subprocesses 

Hope this helps. 

Best,
Shreenith

entry.title: Re: No transfer of interval 4000000000 4000000020 for obj...
entry.content:
That error message means the number range interval for object BU_PARTNER is not correctly transferred or available in your client. In other words, when you try to create a Business Partner, the system is looking for the interval 4000000000 – 4000000020, but it hasn’t been set up or transported properly.

Why it happens
Business Partner number ranges are client-dependent. They don’t get transported automatically with customizing requests. If you migrated or copied settings between clients, the number range interval may not exist in the target client. The grouping you chose (0001 Int Number Assignment) is tied to a specific number range interval. If that interval isn’t defined, you’ll see this error.

How to Fix
Check Number Range Customizing via transaction BUCF, verify that interval 4000000000 – 4000000020 exists for object BU_PARTNER, if not, create it manually.

Check Grouping Assignment
In IMG > SAP Business Partner > Basic Settings > Define Groupings and Assign Number Ranges. Here, ensure, you have maintained the correct range to the required Grouping

Transport Consideration
Remember: number ranges are not transported. You must manually maintain them in each client.

Consistency Check
After maintaining, run BP creation again. The error should disappear once the interval exists and is linked to your grouping.

entry.title: Re: Rework Production order, its component level price di...
entry.content:
This is a classic dismantling / rework costing scenario, and SAP does not provide a direct “split moving average price across BOM components” function out of the box. But you can model it with a combination of production order logic, settlement rules, and valuation strategies.

How it works in real time

Rework / Dismantling Order (CO07)
You create a rework production order for the hypothetical dismantling material. The purchased product (valued at moving average price) is issued to the order. The BOM of the dismantling material contains the components you want to receive back.

Goods Movements
Issue the purchased product to the order (movement 261). Receive the dismantled components back (movement 531). At this point, the order collects the cost of the issued product.

Cost Distribution
The cost of the dismantled product is held in the order. Settlement rules determine how that cost is distributed to the received components. You can configure settlement to distribute costs percentage-wise to each component (based on BOM percentages or manually defined settlement shares).

Settlement (KO88)
When you settle the order, the moving average price of the dismantled product is split according to the percentages and posted to the components. This way, each component gets a share of the dismantled product’s cost, and they can then be sold at that valuation.

entry.title: Re: PM-Revisions not seen in OIOB
entry.content:
Key checks to resolve

Plant Assignment
Revisions are plant‑specific. Make sure the revision you created is tied to the correct plant. If you created it without a plant or with a different plant, OIOB won’t display it when you log in under another plant context.

Validity Dates
Check the validity period of the revision in T352R. If the “valid from” date is in the future, OIOB won’t show it until that date is reached.

Authorization / View Settings
Sometimes, authorizations or user parameters filter what you see in OIOB. Verify that your user has access to the plant and revision group.

Consistency Check
Run transaction OIOB again and ensure you’re in the same client and plant where the entry was created. If still missing, run OIOB → Utilities → Display Table Entries to confirm the customizing view is consistent with T352R.

Transport vs. Local Entry
Revisions are customizing objects. If you created it in one client but are checking in another, it won’t appear unless transported. Double‑check whether you’re in the same client where you made the entry.

entry.title: Re: JMS Mastery Series Part 2 - The JMS Revolution
entry.content:
Great content @MateuszPiotrowski .
One small point regarding Point 6 (“If retries exhausted: Message moves to DLQ”). In CPI, JMS does not have native retry‑exhaustion handling. Messages won’t move to DLQ unless there is a system‑level issue such as a tenant crash or broker recovery failure. For business‑level failures, retry limits must be implemented manually using the JMS retry header.

entry.title: Re: Creating Number Range Objects in SAP BTP Trial Account?
entry.content:
In SAP BTP Trial you cannot create standard Number Range Objects like in on-premise systems using SNRO. Trial accounts have limited admin access so full ABAP dictionary transactions are not available.

Workarounds include using a custom table with logic in your CAP or ABAP application to generate numbers or using database sequences. For production-ready number ranges a full BTP or on-premise system is required.

All numbering in the trial must be handled within your application.

entry.title: Re: How to activate Scope item's in Test System
entry.content:
Dear,

In a 3SL system it's not possible to create any workspace using test system.

All the workspace activities can be done only in Customizing and the content has to be moved from Customizing to Test system through Transports.

Once the "Deploy scope and Organizational Structure" is deployed the content from CBC system is deployed to respective S/4 system and automatically the TR will be generated.

The respective TR has to be released through "Export Customizing Transports" app and has to be imported into the Test system through "Import Collections" app.

Thanks,

SAP support

entry.title: Scheduling of Operations Across Multiple Material Codes
entry.content:
Dear SAP Support Team

We have three material codes for the same model with different operation sequences:

  • HALB0001 – 3 operations
  • HALB0002 – 4 operations
  • HALB0003 – 5 operations

We would like to schedule specific operations for the following work centers: WC0001, WC0002, and WC0004.

Our question is:
Is it possible to perform scheduling for these work centers without splitting the material codes? If yes, kindly guide us on how this can be configured in the system.

For your reference, please find the operation details below:

HALB0001
0010 – WC0001
0020 – WC0002
0030 – WC0005

HALB0002
0010 – WC0003
0020 – WC0004
0030 – WC0006
0040 – WC0007

HALB0003
0010 – WC0008
0020 – WC0009
0030 – WC0004
0040 – WC0002
0050 – WC0004

We appreciate your guidance on the feasibility and recommended approach.

entry.title: Re: Setup VAT deferred tax for Switzerland not available
entry.content: Yes it seems, there is no standard deferred tax solution for Switzerland in S/4HANA Public Cloud today. The practical workaround is to use custom tax codes with interim accounts and transfer VAT at payment, or build a side‑by‑side automation on BTP. For long‑term compliance, it’s best to raise this gap with SAP through the Customer Influence program so Switzerland gets added to the supported countries list.

entry.title: Re: material group
entry.content:
Hi @adam_crane ,
My friend sap won’t automatically let one material “consume” another, but there are standard mechanisms that achieve the effect you want—depending on how strict your business rules are and how much you can adjust master data or BOMs.

My recommended approach (based on your description)
Since you said:
The material group “prevents procurement”
It’s a formality
Another company must fix it but is slow
You want all consumption to use a different material

The cleanest and fastest solution is:
Use Follow‑Up Material or BOM Replacement (CS20)
These require no cooperation from the other company and are fully standard.

entry.title: Re: BAS > DEPLOYMENT "SAPUI5 ABAP Repository with name Z*...
entry.content:
I faced this issue while moving the transport from Development to Quality.

Since I did not deploy the application from Web IDE and instead deployed it using the SE38 program /UI5/UI5_REPOSITORY_LOAD, the images were not moved to the Quality system.

After that, when I tried to deploy through Web IDE, I faced an error Resource UI5_Object /BSPapplication/image/svg does already exist  .

To fix this, I deleted the Ui5RepositoryPathMapping.xml file and then executed the SE38 program /UI5/APP_INDEX_CALCULATE. After running this program, I was able to deploy successfully through Web IDE.

entry.title: Re: Creating Number Range Objects in SAP BTP Trial Account?
entry.content:
Maybe by calling the released class CL_NUMBERRANGE_OBJECTS=>CREATE ?

EDIT: this code works in S/4HANA 2023 FPS01 ON-PREMISE, try it in BTP:

CLASS zcl_nr_demo DEFINITION
  PUBLIC
  FINAL
  CREATE PUBLIC.

  PUBLIC SECTION.
    INTERFACES if_oo_adt_classrun.
ENDCLASS.


CLASS zcl_nr_demo IMPLEMENTATION.
  METHOD if_oo_adt_classrun~main.
    TRY.
        cl_numberrange_objects=>create(
          EXPORTING attributes = VALUE cl_numberrange_objects=>nr_attributes( object       = 'ZDEMO_NR'
                                                                              dtelsobj     = 'SD_NRRANGE_PREFIX'
                                                                              domlen       = 'CHAR10'
                                                                              percentage   = 5
                                                                              buffer       = 'S'
                                                                              noivbuffer   = '1'
                                                                              nrcheckascii = 'X'
                                                                              devclass     = '$TMP' )
                    obj_text   = VALUE cl_numberrange_objects=>nr_obj_text( langu    = sy-langu
                                                                            object   = 'ZDEMO_NR'
                                                                            txt      = 'Demo number range object'
                                                                            txtshort = 'Demo number range ob' )
          IMPORTING errors     = DATA(errors)
                    returncode = DATA(return_code) ).
        IF return_code <> 'E'.
          COMMIT WORK.
          out->write( 'Number Range Object created.' ).
        ELSE.
          ROLLBACK WORK.
          out->write( |Object creation failed: { errors[ 1 ]-msgid }{ errors[ 1 ]-msgnumber }| ).
        ENDIF.

      CATCH cx_number_ranges INTO DATA(lx_obj).
        ROLLBACK WORK.
        out->write( |Object creation failed: { lx_obj->get_text( ) }| ).
    ENDTRY.
  ENDMETHOD.
ENDCLASS.

entry.title: Re: Configurar processo de compra futura do zero
entry.content:
Olá @tjcarvalho ,
Meu amigo, o fluxo MIRO/MIGO do SAP pode parecer confuso para quem está começando, especialmente se a sua empresa quiser personalizá-lo com duas faturas separadas. Deixe-me explicar a lógica, o processo padrão do SAP e o que significa "invertê-lo" da maneira que sua empresa está solicitando.

Primeiro, o Fluxo Padrão de Compras do SAP
No SAP MM, a clássica conciliação de três vias funciona assim:

1. Pedido de Compra (PC)
2. Entrada de Mercadorias (MIGO)

  • Cria o documento de material
  • Atualiza o estoque
  • Lança a compensação GR/IR

3. Recebimento de Fatura (MIRO)

  • Lança a fatura do fornecedor
  • Compensa a GR/IR

Importante:
O SAP espera que o MIGO ocorra antes do MIRO. Isso porque a fatura deve corresponder às mercadorias efetivamente recebidas.

entry.title: Re: material group
entry.content:
In standard SAP, you cannot directly “link” one material number to another so that all consumption postings automatically redirect. The system always consumes the material number that’s on the BOM, production order, or sales order. That said, there are a few ways to model this requirement depending on how flexible your business process can be:

Options to map a procurable alternative

Material Determination (VB11)
You can set up material determination so that when users enter the non‑procurable material in a sales order, the system automatically substitutes it with the procurable material. This works well for sales processes, but not for production order consumption.

Alternative BOM / Phantom Assembly
In production, you can create a BOM where the non‑procurable material is replaced by the procurable one. If the BOM is updated, all consumption will point to the procurable material. Phantom assemblies can also be used to group materials and redirect consumption.

Material Master Substitution (MRP / Procurement)
You can use “Follow‑up Material” or “Alternative Material” settings in the material master to guide MRP to procure the alternative material instead. This affects planning and procurement, but not direct consumption postings.

Batch Management / Classification
If the two materials are essentially interchangeable, you can manage them under a common batch class and substitute at the batch level. This is more complex but can work if you need traceability.

Custom Enhancement (User Exit / BAdI)
If you truly need every consumption posting of Material A to hit Material B’s stock, you’d need a custom enhancement in the goods movement logic (e.g., MB1A/MB1B exits or BAdIs in MIGO). This is not standard and must be carefully controlled to avoid reconciliation issues.

Impact Analysis Criteria – User / Business Role Option Not Editable in Risk Analysis

 

Steps to Reproduce:

  1. Log in to SAP GRC system (DGR).

  2. Open NWBC (NetWeaver Business Client).

  3. Navigate to:

  4. Open an existing Business Role (or create a new one).

  5. Go to Step 2 – Access Risk Analysis.

  6. Click on Run Risk Analysis.

  7. After execution, check the Impact Analysis section.

entry.title: Re: Scheduling of Operations Across Multiple Material Codes
entry.content:
In APO/PPDS, the system schedules based on the operation sequence defined in the PDS (Production Data Structure) that comes from your routing/master recipe. That’s why you currently have three separate material codes — each one represents a different routing with different work center assignments.

Can you schedule without splitting materials?

Directly, no: APO/PPDS requires a unique PDS per material/plant combination. Since each routing has a different sequence of operations, the system sees them as distinct production processes.

Indirectly, yes: You can model this with one material code and multiple routings/master recipes, then transfer them to APO as alternative PDSs. APO can then select the appropriate PDS during planning/scheduling.

Recommended Approaches

Alternative Routings / Recipes in ECC
Maintain multiple routings for the same material (HALBxxxx). Each routing has its own operation sequence (3 ops, 4 ops, 5 ops). Transfer them to APO → you’ll get multiple PDSs for the same material. In APO, you can use production versions or PDS selection conditions to choose which routing to schedule.

Production Versions
In ECC, assign production versions to the material. Each version points to a different routing/BOM combination. When transferred to APO, each production version creates a separate PDS. PPDS can then schedule based on the chosen production version.

PPDS Strategy
You can configure PPDS to select the correct PDS based on planning parameters, order type, or manual planner choice. This way, you don’t need three separate material codes — just one material with multiple production versions.

Practical example for your case

  • Material HALB0001/2/3 could be unified into HALB000X.
  • Create three production versions for HALB000X:
    • Version 1 → Routing with WC0001, WC0002, WC0005
    • Version 2 → Routing with WC0003, WC0004, WC0006, WC0007
    • Version 3 → Routing with WC0008, WC0009, WC0004, WC0002, WC0004
  • Transfer to APO → HALB000X will have three PDSs.
  • Planner or system logic decides which version/PDS to use during scheduling.

entry.title: Impacts of Business Rules modifying Effective Start Date and Suggestions to SAP Team
entry.content:
Dear Readers,

One of my clients had a requirement to modify the effective start date of the MDF (Pay slip MDF) where we modify the effective date of the record based on the type of the payroll run. A Little Context to the Object,  its a multiple changes per day effective dated record where the data is passed via CPI. If Regular , it should be set to 22nd of every month and if Off-cycle , it should be set to payment date passed in the Payload.

Through CPI , we send the generated payslips (via a custom program ) to the SuccessFactors. During Insertion , we received the error (Save error with Index 0) via OData. In the UI , I received a error as stated below

 

com.successfactors.genericobject.app.exception.MDFSQLException: com.sap.db.jdbc.exceptions.SQLIntegrityConstraintViolationExceptionSapDB: [301]: unique constraint violated: Table(GENERIC_OBJECT_T), Index(UK_GENERIC_OBJECT_T_END_DATE) with error: unique constraint violation for table EUSFV4_STOCKPM240373:GENERIC_OBJECT_Ten, constraint='$uc_UK_GENERIC_OBJECT_T_END_DATE$', key value='5066537', pos=0; indexname=UK_GENERIC_OBJECT_T_END_DATE

Later during careful introspection , i found the on save rule modifying the effective start date was the culprit. Refer to the below KBA 

https://userapps.support.sap.com/sap/support/knowledge/en/3452058

I totally get it what SAP means in the KBA and it does makes lot of sense , but restricting it in the business rules solves things at the root level rather than running scripts and unnecessary hassles experienced.

So What you think Experts ,  if SAP should restrict setting the effective start date property in business rules?

entry.title: SAP Business Network for Logistics 2602 – What’s New?
entry.content:
The latest release for SAP Business Network for Logistics delivers a broad set of improvements designed to make scheduling, data management, and cross-center collaboration more reliable and efficient. While several features add valuable capabilities, the highlight of this release is the introduction of recurring blocks for loading points, which unlocks powerful, predictable planning that reduces manual effort and last-minute changes.

Below is a concise tour of what’s new, who it helps, and practical takeaways to start realizing benefits quickly. And at the end, there is an invitation to a ‘Features in Focus’ webinar for shippers and carriers on SAP Business Network for Logistics. Check it out! It’s for free!

SAP Business Network Freight Collaboration

‘Administration Guides for Logistics Service Providers’ and ‘Activate your Account’ guides: language versions to be created manually

The process of downloading PDF versions of translated guides has changed.

Now, you need to manually create PDF language versions of the ’Administration Guide for Logistics Providers’ and ’Activate Your Account’ guides.

To do so, create the translated version on SAP Help Portal and then choose ‘PDF - Create Custom PDF’.

 

Dock Appointment Scheduling: Define daily, weekly or monthly recurring blockers

Recurring blocks allow loading-point time blocks to be defined with daily, weekly, or monthly recurrence. This means you can plan for regular occurrences such as holidays, maintenance windows, or cleaning downtime without recreating blocks each period.

When creating a new recurring block, the system checks for conflicts with existing appointments or blocks to prevent overbooking or double-booking.

Note that recurring blocks cannot be imported from or exported to Microsoft Excel, so planning should be managed within the ‘Manage Entities’ application. The practical benefit is a predictable, automated scheduling rhythm that reduces manual updates and minimizes scheduling surprises during peak periods or special events.

 

For more information, please refer to our additional feature detail deck and SAP Community blog post.

Dock Appointment Scheduling: Define blocks related to max capacity

Previously, users had to manually specify the blocked capacity, and overbooking limits could not be included. With the updated time block feature, shippers can now create blocks that apply to the maximum capacity of a loading point, including overbooking in the ‘Manage Entities’ app. The system checks available capacity when creating time blocks and alerts users if total capacity is exceeded to resolve potential capacity clashes.

 

 This feature helps drive higher utilization of loading-point capacity while keeping service levels intact during peak or constrained periods.

More information on this feature can be found in this SAP Community blog post.

Dock Appointment Scheduling: Microsoft Excel upload / download of loading point data now also supports the fields

The ‘Manage Entities’ app now supports additional fields in Microsoft Excel uploads and downloads. You can include:

  • ‘Use Derived Appointment Duration’ (on a loading point)
  • ‘Max. Capacity’ (on a loading point – block time)
  • ‘Include Overbooking’ (on a loading point – block time)

Dock Appointment Scheduling: based on location defined in TM, loading point can be predefined and cannot be changed

The loading point ID and unloading point ID can be passed from Transportation Management to SAP Business Network for Logistics. This data travels via the ‘Location’ field under ‘Additional Address’ (Source or Destination) in TM.

When the internal ID matches an existing loading point ID, the system preselects and fixes that loading point, preventing changes by shippers or carriers in SAP the ‘Manage Dock Appointments’ and ‘Self-Book Dock Appointments’ apps.

 

Dock Appointment Scheduling: Enhanced appointment validation

The GenericAppointment_In API now triggers an advanced validation of available time blocks and capacity.

If there is no existing time block for the requested slot and enough capacity is available, the system automatically confirms the booking and returns a GenericAppointment_Out with status code '03'.

Conversely, if a time block exists or capacity is insufficient, the system does not confirm the appointment and returns a GenericAppointment_Out with detailed error information.

This gives users clear, actionable feedback and a faster path to a confirmed slot when possible, while maintaining strict accuracy and transparency when issues arise.

 

SAP Business Network Freight Collaboration and SAP Business Network Global Track and Trace

Milestone Reporting: ‘Manage Freight Execution’ app: now supporting cross-data center collaboration

The Manage Freight Execution app now supports cross data center connections, enabling visibility of freight orders and events across data centers. This means shippers’ orders and events are visible to carriers on other data centers, and vice versa, improving collaboration and timeliness of information.

Milestone Reporting: ‘Manage Freight Execution’ app: new filters available on ‘Arrival State’ and ‘Departure State

New filters in ‘Manage Freight Execution’ let users refine searches by ‘Arrival State’ and ‘Departure State’, enabling more precise document discovery based on location status and timing. The benefits are clearer, faster insight and improved cross-organizational coordination.

 

SAP Business Network Freight Collaboration and SAP Business Network, intelligent insights add-on

Harmonization of predefined analytics content

The predefined analytics content for SAP Business Network Freight Collaboration was previously available as

  • Predefined stories in the ‘Manage Analytic Stories’ app for use with embedded analytics
  • Business contents that can be imported into your SAP Analytics Cloud tenant

To reduce duplication between the two, as of November 15, 2025, the business content was already deprecated, and you can't import them into SAP Analytics Cloud. With this release, you can’t display the imported business content in SAP Analytics Cloud dashboards. Instead, you can use story dashboards within intelligent insights that integrates embedded analytics toolsets for data analysis.

This shift reduces duplication and aligns analytics with the embedded analytics approach.

 

Announcement for shippers and carriers on SAP Business Network for Logistics

Join our upcoming webinar to discover how both shippers and carriers can seamlessly integrate, collaborate, and optimize their end-to-end logistics processes using REST APIs. Don’t miss this opportunity to unlock the full power of SAP Business Network for Logistics!

This session will be held on March 4th, 2026. It is expected to last approximately 45 minutes and will also be recorded.

To secure your spot for this informative webinar, please visit the below registration link, according to your region. Once registered, and you are unable to attend, you will receive the recording of the webinar to enjoy in your own time. 

Choose the time that works best for you (same content in both sessions): 

Click here to register for Option 1 on March 4th 11:00am IST / 2:30pm JST / 4:30pm AEDT

Click here to register for Option 2 on March 4th 11:00am EST / 1:00pm BRT / 4:00pm GMT

 

--------------------

The above summarizes the key innovations for 2602. We hope you enjoy the new capabilities. Thanks for reading the blog post and stay tuned! 

A full overview of the 2602 release can be found under the following link.

To learn more about SAP Business Network for Logistics visit SAP - Business Network for Logistics.

For further questions and feedback related to the blog, please check the Q&A area and feel free to post your questions in the link with the tag SAP Business Network for Logistics.

entry.title: SAP BI 2025: Why RichText is Richer Than You Think
entry.content:
The SAP BusinessObjects BI 2025 release introduces the RichText interactive element, a powerful new addition to Web Intelligence that transforms how you present textual content in your reports.

What Can You Do with RichText?

🎨Effortless Formatting

Design text to display like articles or enriched presentation. The intuitive toolbar gives you quick access to essential styling options: fonts, colors, alignments, and bullet lists—all without any code knowledge.

  • Typography controls – fonts, sizes, and weights
  • Visual styling – colors, highlights, and emphasis
  • Layout options – alignments, bullet lists, and spacing
  • Paste – External HTML content can be imported. All content is sanitized for security purposes.

 

Dynamic Data Integration

RichText seamlessly integrates with your business data through Dynamic Data Objects. Simply drag and drop objects into the RichText feeding pane, just as you would with any other report element.

To insert dynamic data:

  1. Click the Insert Objects button
  2. Select your desired business object
  3. Customize the display format to match your needs

Quick Examples:

A single aggregated value as KPI:


Handling multiple values? Navigate to the Multiple Values section in settings, where you can define custom separators—semicolons, commas, or even HTML tags—to format dynamic lists exactly as you envision them.





Similar example using HTML separator:

 

 

 


 

The Hidden Power of RichText

RichText delivers far more than basic text formatting. Combine live data with HTML5 capabilities, and your creativity becomes the only boundary.

🌐 HTML & CSS Integration

Leverage modern web standards to create advanced layouts and custom styling that bring contemporary design directly into Web Intelligence.

Real-world example: Imagine adding a dynamic, context-sensitive tooltip at the top of your report that provides rich details on-demand—without cluttering your tables or charts.

In the video example, mouse hover triggers tooltips displaying live data:

 

How to Create such Interactive Tooltips?

  1. Add the RichText Element Drop a new RichText element onto your report canvas.
  2. Feed Your Data Add at least one dimension as an object in the Feeding pane, or drag it directly onto the element in the canvas.
    • In this example, we're using the [Lines] dimension.
  •  Switch to HTML Mode In the RichText-specific settings pane, switch to HTML mode and remove the default separator character—leave it blank.

  • Insert the HTML Code Paste this HTML snippet:
<div class="hover-wrap">Show Top 5 Products<div class="table-column"><div class="table-head">Best Products</div>{[Lines]}</div></div>
  • Access Advanced Settings Select the fifth tab labeled "Advanced Settings."
  • Add the CSS Styling Paste the following CSS code:
overflow: visible;
cursor: default;

.hover-wrap {
  padding: 6px 10px;
  border: 1px solid #d0d7e2;
  border-radius: 8px;
  position: relative;
  display: inline-block;
  overflow: visible;
}

.table-column {
  position: absolute;
  left: 0;
  top: calc(100% + 6px);
  width: auto;
  min-width: 220px;
  max-width: min(90vw, 360px);
  z-index: 9999;
  border: 1px solid #d7dce3;
  border-radius: 10px;
  background: #fff;
  box-shadow: 0 10px 28px rgba(1, 23, 42, 0.2);
  overflow: visible;
  opacity: 0;
  transform: translateY(8px);
  pointer-events: none;
  transition: opacity 180ms ease, transform 180ms ease;
  margin-top: 0;
}

.hover-wrap:hover .table-column {
  opacity: 1;
  transform: translateY(0);
}

.table-head {
  padding: 6px 10px;
  border-top-left-radius: 10px;
  border-top-right-radius: 10px;
  background: linear-gradient(135deg, #2563eb, #3b82f6);
  color: #fff;
  font-weight: 600;
}

.table-column span {
  display: block;
  padding: 4px 8px;
  border-bottom: 1px solid #e5e9f0;
  background: #f9fafb;
  white-space: normal;
  word-break: break-word;
}

.table-column span:nth-child(even) {
  background: #f2f5fb;
}

.table-column span:last-child {
  border-bottom: none;
}

 

Test Your Tooltip Hover your mouse over the RichText element in the canvas. You should now see a beautifully decorated tooltip appear on screen with your live data!

 

Ready to Explore?

The RichText interactive element is available now in SAP BusinessObjects Web Intelligence 2025. Give it a try and see how it transforms your reporting experience.

RichText's HTML and CSS integration opens up endless possibilities for interactive reporting in SAP BI. Interactive tooltips are merely the starting point. The real magic happens when you combine your creativity with live data to transform static reports into engaging, dynamic experiences for consumers.

entry.title: Re: Building a Real RAP Application Step by Step — Vlog Series Introduction
entry.content:
Thank you so much for this clear explanation, Alejandro!
I followed the steps exactly as shown, and it worked perfectly in my BTP Trial account.
Really appreciate the practical walkthrough  this was extremely helpful. 🙌

entry.title: Unlocking Strategic Pricing with SAP Advanced Commodity Pricing Engine
entry.content:
In today’s global markets, commodities are no longer just raw materials—they’re strategic assets. Volatile prices, complex contracts, and multiple market indices make commodity management a high-stakes challenge for companies in energy, agriculture, metals and manufacturing. Enter SAP Advanced Commodity Pricing Engine a core component of SAP Commodity Management, providing a powerful tool designed to simplify, automate, and optimize commodity pricing.

What is SAP Advanced Commodity Pricing Engine?

The Advanced Commodity Pricing Engine is a sophisticated module in SAP that enables companies to calculate commodity prices dynamically based on market indices, contracts, and predefined rules. Unlike static pricing methods, Advanced Commodity Pricing Engine can handle complex pricing formulas, variable discounts, secondary cost adjustments, and quality differentials, giving businesses a single source of truth for commodity costs.

Key highlights include:

  • Automated price calculations based on market benchmarks
  • Integration with SAP S/4HANA for seamless procurement, sales, and finance processes
  • Flexibility to handle diverse commodities like crude oil, refined fuel products, metals, grains, and chemical products
  • Drives commodity risk exposure to facilitate risk management and mitigation

Why Businesses Need Advanced Commodity Pricing Engine

Commodity price fluctuations are unpredictable. Companies that rely on manual pricing processes face several challenges:

  • Risk of errors and inconsistencies across regions
  • Delays in price updates, leading to financial losses
  • Difficulty in tracking contract obligations against market movements

Advanced Commodity Pricing Engine addresses these challenges by:

  1. Standardizing pricing logic across all commodities
  2. Reducing operational complexity with automated calculations
  3. Enabling proactive risk management by linking prices to market indices
  4. Enhancing transparency for audit and compliance purposes

Core Features of Advanced Commodity Pricing Engine

  1. Dynamic Price Formulas
    Configure formulas that automatically calculate prices using market indices, secondary charges, and quality adjustments.
  2. Index-Based Pricing
    Link your contracts to global benchmarks (e.g., Brent crude, LME metals, CME wheat futures) for real-time market-reflective pricing.
  3. Flexible Adjustments
    Incorporate location-specific logistics, quality differentials, and taxes into price calculations automatically.
  4. Scenario Simulation
    Run “what-if” scenarios to assess the impact of price fluctuations on margins and contracts before they occur.  
  5. Seamless Integration
    Works with SAP S/4HANA’s procurement, sales, finance, and logistics modules, ensuring a unified view of commodity costs across the e2e supply chain.

What’s new with Advanced Commodity Pricing Engine

The new Advanced Commodity Pricing Engine offers the following enhancements:

  • A new RAP business object R_CmmdtyPrcgConditionFormulaTP for formula data model and a new RAP business object R_CmmdtyPrcgPriceFixationSetTP for price fixation groups of future basis pricing process have been included.
  • A new CPE interface CL_CMM_CPE_INTERFACE_BO allows you to update CPE data in batch scenarios through BAPI.
  • Changes to the allocation data can be made manually through the allocation workbench (CMM_ALLOC). With this functionality, you will now be able to switch the mode from automatic allocation to manual.
  • Price quotations are now visible in display mode for logistic documents. This allows you to revisit the quotations from the last time it was updated.
  • All the pricing aspects in logistic documents are now saved locally within the document. With this functionality, the changes are isolated to the specific document.
  • Optimized Future/Basis Pricing scenarios.
  • Considerable performance improvement for mass processing scenarios with high data volume.

Benefits of Implementing Advanced Commodity Pricing Engine

  • New strategic engine: where all new/enhanced capabilities and innovations would be incorporated in the future
  • Enhanced Transactional data consistency: Configuration changes in formula do not impact existing contracts using defaulted formula details.
  • Operational Efficiency: Automates complex pricing, reducing manual intervention and errors.
  • Financial Accuracy: Real-time market pricing ensures accurate P&L reporting and cost allocation.
  • Strategic Advantage: Quickly adapt to market changes, optimizing procurement, hedging, and sales strategies.
  • Regulatory Compliance: Transparent pricing formulas and audit trails help meet internal and external reporting requirements.
  • Clean Core: Standard extensibility options such as key user and developer extensibility is supported with the introduction of the RAP (ABAP RESTful Application Programming Model) data model.
  • Cloud APIs: RAP re-use component offers cloud consumable APIs.
  • Increased end user satisfaction: due to enhanced performance and system response time.

Who Should Use Advanced Commodity Pricing Engine?

Companies dealing with:

  • Energy Commodities: Oil, refined products, biofuels
  • Agriculture & Consumer Products: Grains, cocoa, coffee, sugar
  • Metals & Mining: Base metals, precious metals, concentrates, coal
  • Chemicals & Raw Materials: Fertilizers, polymers, specialty chemicals

If your business deals with high-volume commodities, dynamic market conditions, or complex contracts, Advanced Commodity Pricing Engine can transform the way you manage pricing.

Conclusion - a powerful tool designed to simplify, automate, and optimize commodity pricing.

In volatile commodity markets, price accuracy isn’t just a convenience—it’s a competitive advantage. SAP Advanced Commodity Pricing Engine empowers organizations to calculate, automate, and optimize commodity prices while integrating seamlessly into existing SAP workflows.

By adopting Advanced Commodity Pricing Engine, businesses can reduce operational risk, improve financial transparency, and stay agile in the face of market fluctuations—turning commodity pricing from a challenge into a strategic tool.

For more information please follow this link to help.sap.com: help.sap.com - Advanced Commodity Pricing Engine

Stay tuned for future innovations by following SAP Roadmap Explorer  for Commodity Management: https://roadmaps.sap.com/board?PRODUCT=42F2E964F4E71EDAA6A3587248CD40E5&range=CURRENT-LAST

entry.title: Re: Open Posting Periods for Specific Ledgers Only
entry.content:
See help documentation on Alternative Fiscal Year Variant

See also community blog 

https://community.sap.com/t5/enterprise-resource-planning-blog-posts-by-sap/in-depth-with-sap-s-4hana-ledger-scoping-q-amp-a/ba-p/13571167

In particular see 

Question 38:
Can I use the fiscal year variant to close periods differently for each ledger?
Answer:
Yes, it is recommended to define a different posting period variant if an alternative fiscal year variant (AFYV) is used.
Note that if an AFYV is assigned to the non-leading ledger, you need a different posting period. Opening and closing posting periods is defined in the posting period variants and can differ between leading and non-leading ledger.

Question 39:
Can I have different closing periods for the leading ledger and the non-leading ledger? For example, to close the non-leading ledger first and leave only the leading ledger open for additional entries such as GAAP conversion or audit adjustment.
Answer:
Adjustments are recorded for the non-leading ledger. All postings in the system are recorded for leading and non-leading ledger.
So, closing the non-leading ledger will not really have an impact for the leading ledger.
If you have an alternative fiscal year variant assigned to the non-leading ledger, you should define a corresponding posting period variant.
This will allow you to close the periods separately in the leading ledger and the non-leading ledger.

See also Posting Periods

entry.title: Custom Cube for Margin Analysis - Calculated Key Figure shows no data
entry.content:
Hello Experts,

i posted this question initially in another Q&A Forum because i was not sure if this Learning Group is the right place to ask questions.

But since i recieved no answer to my question i will post the question in this Learning Group again.

Some background information:

Back in R/3 we used KE35 (Form) and KER1 (Key Figure Scheme) to prepare for KE30 (Profitability Report) in SAP CO-PA modul.

So far i have done significant tests only with transient view entity … analytical query and was able to replace our custom reports from R/3 costing based COPA to S/4 Margin Analysis queries. The results are very good and V3 consumption views are nice to use.

However, i noticed that using a custom consumption view offers very little reuseability. A new requirement triggers a new consumption view and copy/paste of the CDS view. So i want to achieve REUSEABILITY for restricted measures and calculated measures. Looking at the SAP VDM i think it is time to build custom cubes, which i successfully circumvented so far.

So how can i replace R/3 „Forms“, „Key Figures Schemes“ and „Reports“ with Embedded Analytics? I am trying to do a crossover to the VDM which uses cubes, querys, restricted measures, and calculated measures. (Screenshot shows R/3 setup of CO-PA reporting)

I am not trying to replicate the old world 1 by 1, but take over what is still useful e.g. „Prepare a Form“ and „Prepare Key Figures“ and reuse this in multiple querys.

I created the above „custom VDM“ and while the values of the restricted measures are shown correct (see below, RSRT) in the consumption view, the calcuated measures are always inital. What am i doing wrong?

Formulas are very simple:

Revenue - COGS = ContributionMargin1 and FIDirect - OverhAlloc = FIMinusOverhead

My guess is that the calculated measures may have lost their dimensions? Could that be the reason?

Or do i not understand how aggregation works?

Greatful for any tipps. I can share the CDS Views if needed.

Thank you

Regards

David

entry.title: Re: Guidance on best adapter choice for inbound integrati...
entry.content:
Hello,

Thank you very much for your question. I'm very sorry for the delay.

In this case, I asked one of your internal chatbots for support. It referred to the following information sources:

SAP S/4HANA Cloud ABAP Pipeline vs Cloud Data Integration

Extract Data from SAP S/4HANA Cloud and Integrate

Here's the response. I hope that it helps:

When considering inbound integration scenarios using SAP Cloud Integration, especially for creating and updating business objects in SAP Cloud Private, several adapter options are available. Here is a breakdown of the most commonly recommended adapters and considerations for each:

1. OData Services

  • Advantages: OData services are standardized, REST-based, and support CRUD operations, making them ideal for integration with web or mobile applications. They are particularly suitable if your backend is built using modern paradigms such as RAP/CDS, as OData naturally aligns with these technologies.
  • Considerations: OData is recommended when you need a flexible, scalable solution that can easily integrate with various front-end technologies.

2. IDoc

  • Advantages: IDocs are robust and well-suited for batch processing and large data volumes. They are commonly used in SAP-to-SAP integrations.
  • Considerations: IDocs might not be the best choice for real-time or synchronous operations due to their asynchronous nature.

3. RFC/BAPI

  • Advantages: RFCs and BAPIs are powerful for direct SAP system interactions and can handle complex business logic.
  • Considerations: These are more suitable for SAP-to-SAP integrations and might require more effort to expose to non-SAP systems.

4. SOAP

  • Advantages: SOAP is a well-established protocol that supports complex transactions and provides built-in error handling.
  • Considerations: SOAP can be more complex to implement and maintain compared to REST-based services like OData.

When Not to Use OData

  • Performance Concerns: If the integration involves very high data volumes or requires complex transactions, OData might not be the best choice.
  • Legacy Systems: If the external system does not support RESTful services, other protocols like SOAP or IDoc might be more appropriate.

Long-term Sustainability

  • OData: Offers good extensibility and is easier to maintain due to its alignment with modern web standards.
  • IDoc/RFC/BAPI: These might require more specialized knowledge to maintain but are very stable for SAP-centric environments.

In conclusion, OData is often the preferred choice for modern, flexible integrations, especially when the backend is built using RAP/CDS. However, the choice of adapter should be based on specific project requirements, including performance, compatibility, and long-term maintenance considerations.

Kind regards,

Henrike

entry.title: Re: Operations not getting created from BAPI_ROUTING_CREA...
entry.content:
Hello,

Thank you very much for your question. I'm very sorry for the delay.

In this case, I asked an internal chatbot for support. Here's the response:

To resolve the issue where operations are not getting created when using the BAPI ‘BAPI_ROUTING_CREATE’, you should ensure that the data being passed is complete and correctly formatted. Here are a few steps to consider:

  1. Check Mandatory Fields: Ensure that all mandatory fields for operations are filled correctly. This includes fields like ACTIVITY, OPERATION_ID, CONTROL_KEY, WORK_CNTR, and PLANT.

  2. Sequence and Group Counter: Verify that the SEQUENCE_NO and GROUP_COUNTER are correctly formatted and match the expected format in the system. Leading zeros might be necessary depending on the system configuration.

  3. Data Consistency: Ensure that the data in lt_opr (operations table) is consistent with the data in lt_tsk (task list table) and lt_seq (sequence table). The TASK_LIST_GROUP and GROUP_COUNTER should match across these tables.

  4. Test in SE37: Since the data works in SE37, compare the data being passed in the BAPI call with the data used in SE37 to identify any discrepancies.

  5. Check for Errors: After the BAPI call, check the lt_return table for any error messages that might indicate why the operations are not being created.

  6. Transaction Commit: Ensure that the BAPI transaction is committed correctly using BAPI_TRANSACTION_COMMIT after the BAPI call.

I hope this helps. 

Kind regards,

Henrike

entry.title: Re: Looking for Reliable and Updated Practice Questions f...
entry.content:
Dear @petirklook,

Point 1. Verify the exam is the right one and still the one you’ll take

  • Use SAP Learning Get certified to locate your exact certification and open the related learning journey + exam entry
  • Also check SAP retirement rules in case an exam is being phased out
  • SAP is actively transitioning certifications to practical/performance-based formats in phases so the best source of truth is always what your Certification page shows right now

More details below:

https://learning.sap.com/get-certified

https://learning.sap.com/helpcenter/certification-support/certification-retirements

Point 2. Official learning content

These two official academies are explicitly positioned as preparation for C_TS4FI_2023:

  • TS4F01 Financial Accounting in SAP S/4HANA (Academy Part I)
  • TS4F02 Financial Accounting in SAP S/4HANA (Academy Part II)

For additional official courses you can mix in (e-learning / instructor-led) use SAP Training Financial Accounting training path as your catalog

More details below:

https://training.sap.com/trainingpath/Applications-Financial+Accounting-SAP+S4HANA

Point 3. Official SAP Learning Journeys

SAP certification flow explicitly points learners to Learning Journeys as the preparation path

More details below:

https://learning.sap.com/helpcenter/certification-support/how-to-get-certified

A relevant official journey to use as a structured checklist (FI core areas) is:

  • Implementing Financial Accounting in SAP S/4HANA Cloud

More details below:

https://learning.sap.com/learning-journeys/implementing-financial-accounting-in-sap-s-4hana?searchId=193f19e7-c1a5-4b2b-9ee5-bb315e1ccc21&listPosition=1

Point 4. Practice questions / mock tests what SAP officially provides

SAP generally does not publish a public official question bank for associate exams. The official alternatives are:

  • Hands-on practice systems bundled with certification offerings:
    • SAP Certification exam, two attempts includes 10 hours of access to certification-relevant hands-on practice systems
  • SAP Learning Hub which includes hands-on practice systems and four certification exam attempts per year per subscription terms
  • Good book from SAP PRESS below:

https://www.sap-press.com/sap-s4hana-financial-accounting-certification-guide_5310/?srsltid=AfmBOooQzosm7vVH9RXnoufALO4iHdTZXsBszkXqXEV8RzUZtGmIU22i

Best Regards,

Dawid
